_readme: Haspataal MCP Server Configuration — copy relevant servers to your Claude settings
_usage: Add mcpServers entries to ~/.claude.json or project .claude/settings.json
_context_warning: Keep under 10 MCPs active to preserve your 200k context window

supabase.description: Supabase MCP — query tables, run migrations, manage RLS policies, inspect schema. Use for haspataal DB operations.

github.description: GitHub MCP — create PRs, review code, manage issues, search the haspataal repo.

filesystem.description: Filesystem MCP — read/write files in the haspataal project root. Use for bulk file operations.

postgres.description: Direct PostgreSQL MCP — run raw SQL, check query plans via EXPLAIN ANALYZE, inspect RLS policies. Use with postgres-patterns skill.

memory.description: Persistent memory MCP — store and recall cross-session context. Use for remembering ongoing tasks and WAR ROOM decisions.

vercel.description: Vercel MCP — manage deployments. Enable when deploying to production.

redis.description: Redis MCP — inspect appointment slot cache and session data. Enable for cache debugging.

_setup_instructions.step1: Get your Supabase service role key from: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/deqcehtgwbpmwlajsore/settings/api
_setup_instructions.step2: Create a GitHub Personal Access Token at: https://github.com/settings/tokens (repo scope)
_setup_instructions.step3: Add to your environment or .env: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=... GITHUB_TOKEN=...
_setup_instructions.step4: Copy the mcpServers section to your Claude project settings or ~/.claude.json
_setup_instructions.step5: Keep only haspataal_recommended servers active to preserve context window
